subject: Public Health
title: Mental Health Legislation
description: A quick win: understand Mental Health Legislation.

hook.text: In 2023, 40% of countries still had no dedicated mental health legislation—leaving patients without legal protections.

buildup.text: Mental health laws protect patient rights, regulate involuntary treatment, and mandate insurance coverage.

discovery.text: Parity laws—requiring equal insurance coverage for mental and physical health—have expanded access where enacted.

twist.text: Even with parity laws, enforcement is weak—insurers find ways to limit mental health benefits in practice.

climax.text: The UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities calls for community-based mental health care for all.

punchline.text: Rights on paper mean nothing without enforcement in practice.

quiz.question: What do mental health parity laws require?
options: Equal insurance coverage for mental and physical health
Free mental health care for everyone
